specific_location: in the back yard 5 miles south of Lamar on Highway 287 and a little over a quarter of mile to the west
description: -overall appearance a little heavier than many warblers  -greenish olive on back and topside of wings without any distinct markings -blackish crown and auriculars with bright yellow distinctive spectacles -all bright yellow underneath from throat to undertail coverts  -pinkish legsBehaviors: -behavior was atypical for a secretive warbler -first feeding below rock walls in weeds on the side of the ensilage pit -then openly feeding along edge of flower beds along sidewalk -photos were taken through the glass on the door as it strolled down the sidewalk completely in the open -if the door was open, he may have walked right in -perfect opportunity for positive idCall: silentPlumage: not full adult male?
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp
